query: SpaceX IPO June 2026 latest news valuation pricing date Nasdaq SPCX
answer:
I now have a very comprehensive dataset from repository research files. Here is my full synthesis:

---

# SpaceX IPO Research Report — As of June 2, 2026

## Executive Summary

SpaceX (ticker: **SPCX**) has filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC and is targeting a **June 12, 2026 listing on the Nasdaq**. The combined entity—spanning launch services, Starlink, xAI/Grok, and the X social media platform—is seeking a valuation of **$1.25–$1.75 trillion** and aims to raise approximately **$75 billion**, which would make it the largest IPO in history. The S-1 was publicly filed on May 20, 2026, and the roadshow was expected to begin around June 4, 2026 (per the 15-day JOBS Act rule).

> **Data Vintage Warning:** The underlying research files were last updated on **May 24, 2026** (9 days ago). Live web search tools were unavailable, so developments between May 25 and June 2 (roadshow launch, pricing range amendment, any schedule changes) could not be confirmed.

---

## IPO Timeline

| Date | Event | Source |
|------|-------|--------|
| March 30, 2026 | Confidential Draft Registration Statement (DRS) filed with SEC | SEC EDGAR |
| April 1, 2026 | Public reports of confidential filing | NYT, Investopedia, Teslarati |
| April 7–14, 2026 | Reuters exclusive: SpaceX details IPO plans, targets early June roadshow | Reuters |
| May 4, 2026 | 5-for-1 stock split executed | S-1 filing |
| May 14, 2026 | CNBC reports prospectus imminent | CNBC |
| May 15, 2026 | Reuters exclusive: SpaceX targets **June 12 listing** | Reuters |
| May 17, 2026 | BlackRock potentially investing up to $10B | Barron's, MSN |
| **May 20, 2026** | **S-1 prospectus filed publicly** (270+ pages, 226 MB, 110 documents) | SEC EDGAR |
| ~June 4, 2026 | Earliest roadshow start (15-day JOBS Act rule) | Regulatory |
| June 11, 2026 | Retail investor participation deadline (per Gotrade) | Gotrade |
| **June 12, 2026** | **Target listing date on Nasdaq** | Reuters, multiple sources |

---

## Ticker, Exchange, and Listing Details

- **Ticker Symbol:** SPCX
- **Exchange:** Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas
- **Company Status:** "Controlled company" under Nasdaq rules (Elon Musk controls 85% of votes via Class B shares)
- **Underwriters:** Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC Capital Markets, UBS, Wells Fargo, Allen & Company

---

## Valuation & Capital Raise

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Target IPO valuation range | **$1.25–$1.75 trillion** |
| Upper-end estimates (prediction markets) | Above **$2 trillion** |
| Target capital raise | **~$75 billion** (largest IPO in history) |
| Most recent RSA grant-date fair value (post-split) | $93.87/share |
| Pro forma shares outstanding | ~12.5 billion |
| Implied equity at RSA fair value | ~$1.18 trillion |
| Previous private valuation (late 2024) | ~$180–210 billion |
| Pre-merger private valuation (widely reported) | ~$350 billion |

---

## Corporate Structure (Three-Segment Combined Entity)

SpaceX is going public as a merged entity following two major acquisitions:
1. **March 28, 2025:** X Holdings Corp acquired by xAI
2. **February 2, 2026:** xAI Holdings Corp acquired by SpaceX

### Business Segments:
- **Space** — Launch services (Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, Starship), Dragon spacecraft
- **Connectivity** — Starlink broadband (10.3M subscribers, ~9,600 LEO satellites)
- **AI** — xAI (Grok AI), X platform (~550M MAUs), COLOSSUS data center (1 GW)

Starlink is **not** being spun off separately; it goes public as the "Connectivity" segment of SpaceX.

---

## Key Financial Data (from S-1 Filing)

### Revenue (USD millions)

| Period | Space | Connectivity | AI | **Total** |
|--------|-------|-------------|-----|-----------|
| Q1 2026 | $1,286 | $2,590 | $818 | **$4,694** |
| FY 2025 | $4,086 | $11,387 | $3,201 | **$18,674** |
| FY 2024 | $3,796 | $7,599 | $2,620 | **$14,015** |
| FY 2023 | $3,557 | $3,869 | $2,961 | **$10,387** |

### Net Income / (Loss)

| Period | Amount |
|--------|--------|
| Q1 2026 | $(4,276)M loss |
| FY 2025 | $(4,937)M loss |
| FY 2024 | $791M income |
| FY 2023 | $(4,628)M loss |

### Balance Sheet (March 31, 2026)

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Total assets | $102.1 billion |
| Cash | $15.9 billion |
| Total equity | $34.5 billion |
| Total liabilities | $60.5 billion |
| Bridge loan | $20.0 billion |
| Backlog (Dec 31, 2025) | $28.4 billion |

### Capital Expenditures (FY 2025): **$20.7 billion** ($3.8B Space, $4.2B Connectivity, $12.7B AI)

---

## Operating Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Starlink subscribers (Q1 2026) | 10.3 million (up from 5.0M Q1 2025) |
| Starlink ARPU | $66/month (down from $86/month Q1 2025) |
| Active Starlink satellites | ~9,600 in LEO |
| Rocket launches (FY 2025) | 170 |
| Rocket launches (Q1 2026) | 40 |
| Cumulative Starship investment | $15+ billion |
| Connectivity segment EBITDA (FY 2025) | $7.2 billion |
| X platform MAUs | ~550 million |

---

## Governance

- **Dual-class structure:** Class A (1 vote/share, public) / Class B (10 votes/share, insiders)
- **Elon Musk controls 85% of votes**, serves as CEO, CTO, and Chairman simultaneously
- **Musk compensation:** 1 billion restricted shares tied to achieving $7.5T market cap AND a permanent Mars colony with 1 million inhabitants

---

## Analyst & Market Commentary

- Jim Cramer warned the IPO could be "destructive" for the broader market
- Analysts debate whether mega-IPOs (SpaceX + OpenAI) could signal a market top
- Space ETFs surging in anticipation
- Barron's warned it could pressure Tesla stock as Musk attention shifts
- If valued at $1.75T, Musk could become the world's first trillionaire (BBC, ABC Australia)
- Morningstar described xAI's financials as "reckless"
- Record retail investor allocation planned—a departure from typical IPO structures

---

## Notable Related-Party Transactions

- $700M spent on Tesla Megapacks (2024–2025)
- $131M on Tesla Cybertrucks (~1,183–1,813 vehicles, ~6–9% of all Cybertruck sales)
- Option to acquire **Cursor** (AI code editor) at $60B implied equity value in Class A stock, post-IPO

---

## Key Unknowns (as of June 2, 2026)

The research data is 9 days old. Critical events that may have occurred since May 24:
1. Whether the **S-1/A amendment with pricing range** has been filed
2. Whether the **roadshow has launched** (expected ~June 4)
3. Whether the **June 12 listing date** remains on track
4. Any updates on **BlackRock's $10B commitment**
5. Whether SpaceX has appeared on **Nasdaq's official IPO calendar**

---

key_facts: SpaceX has filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC on May 20, 2026 (CIK: 0001181412), making an IPO imminent
Target listing date is June 12, 2026 on Nasdaq under ticker symbol SPCX
Target IPO valuation range is $1.25-$1.75 trillion, with prediction markets pricing above $2 trillion
Target capital raise is approximately $75 billion, which would be the largest IPO in history
SpaceX is going public as a combined three-segment entity: Space (launches), Connectivity (Starlink), and AI (xAI/Grok/X platform)
A 5-for-1 stock split was executed on May 4, 2026, resulting in ~12.5 billion pro forma shares outstanding
FY 2025 total revenue was $18.674 billion across all segments; Q1 2026 revenue was $4.694 billion
Starlink has 10.3 million subscribers as of Q1 2026, up from 5.0 million a year earlier
SpaceX reported net losses of $4.937 billion in FY 2025 and $4.276 billion in Q1 2026 alone
Elon Musk controls 85% of shareholder votes through a dual-class share structure (Class B = 10 votes/share)
Underwriters include Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, and others
BlackRock reportedly may invest up to $10 billion in the IPO
SpaceX merged with xAI Holdings Corp (Feb 2, 2026) and X Holdings Corp (via xAI, March 28, 2025) prior to the IPO
Roadshow expected to begin around June 4, 2026 per the 15-day JOBS Act rule from the May 20 S-1 filing
Total assets of $102.1 billion and total liabilities of $60.5 billion (including $20B bridge loan) as of March 31, 2026
Musk's compensation includes 1 billion restricted shares tied to $7.5T market cap and Mars colony milestones
SpaceX has an option to acquire Cursor (AI code editor) at $60 billion implied equity post-IPO
Data vintage: research files last updated May 24, 2026; developments after that date could not be verified due to tool limitations
